sketchMechanisms: Option::unwrap on None causes a panic with the message 'called `Option::unwrap()` on a `None` value' — the act of demanding something be present when it is absent destroys the program
TypeScript's 'never' type represents values that can never occur — a function returning 'never' can never successfully complete, yet the type system allows you to assign it a name and call it
A LEFT JOIN preserves every row from the left table even when no match exists on the right, filling the missing side with NULL — the join keeps one party whole while the other dissolves into absence
